title: minuartia tenuifolia subsp. hybrida
intro: Minuartia tenuifolia subsp. hybrida, also known as alpine wintergreen, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Alpine wintergreen has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. Alpine wintergreen can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
